name: NXTL
verdict: Pay-as-you-go pioneer — no bundles, no expiry
exitCountry: Unverified (claims VPN-style routing via own infra)

throttlePolicy.notes: NXTL advertises 'VPN-style routing through NXTL infrastructure' which may bypass the GFW, but no independent China field test found as of July 2026. Pay-as-you-go model: $11.99 buys the eSIM + $10 usable balance; China per-GB rate requires their online calculator to find — not published on a simple pricing page. No throttling policy published.

appAccess.notes: Claimed via VPN-style routing — unverified by field test

pros: No expiry — balance rolls over indefinitely across trips
Pay only for what you use — no wasted data on short trips
One eSIM covers 190+ countries, no rebuy needed
cons: China per-GB price hidden behind calculator — not transparent at a glance
GFW bypass unconfirmed by independent China field test
Smaller brand — less community testing data available
